title: Dog Chewing on Foot Until Raw: Causes, Triage, and Next Steps
meta_desc: Find out why your dog is chewing their feet raw, if it is a veterinary emergency, and how taking a photo can help with professional triage.
content_html:
What causes a dog to chew their foot until it is raw?

When a dog chews their paws excessively, it is typically a response to intense itching or pain. Common culprits include environmental allergies to grass or pollen, food sensitivities, or external parasites like fleas and mites. Sometimes, a dog may chew due to a physical injury, such as a thorn embedded in the pad or a broken nail. If the behavior persists, it can lead to a condition called a lick granuloma, which is a chronic sore caused by repetitive licking.

Is this a veterinary emergency?

The urgency level for a dog chewing their foot until raw is Medium. While it is rarely an immediate life-threatening emergency, it is a significant welfare concern because it indicates your pet is in distress or pain. Raw skin is also highly vulnerable to secondary bacterial or yeast infections. You should aim to see a veterinarian within 24 to 48 hours, or sooner if you notice swelling, discharge, or if your dog is unable to put weight on the limb.

Why taking a photo helps with triage

Capturing a clear, high-resolution photo of the raw area is one of the most helpful things you can do for a triage professional. A photo allows a veterinarian to assess the severity of the tissue damage, identify signs of infection (such as pustules or extreme redness), and look for foreign bodies without causing the dog further stress by over-handling the painful limb. This visual information helps determine the priority of your appointment and ensures the right care plan is started immediately.

faqs.question: Should I stop my dog from chewing immediately?
faqs.answer: Yes. Using an Elizabethan collar (cone) is the most effective way to prevent further self-injury and allow the skin to begin healing before your vet appointment.

faqs.question: Can I wash the raw area with soap?
faqs.answer: You can gently rinse the area with lukewarm water or a mild, pet-safe antiseptic solution, but avoid harsh human soaps or alcohols which can sting and worsen irritation.

faqs.question: Is it okay to use human anti-itch creams?
faqs.answer: No. Many human medications, especially those containing zinc or certain steroids, can be toxic if your dog licks them off. Always consult a vet before applying topical treatments.
